chunks.text: 2023-24 Archive of Special Events - EECS at Berkeley 2023-24 Archive of Special Events EECS Department Chair Claire Tomlin and EE Division Chair Clark Nguyen Passing the Baton During a ceremony recognizing the service and contributions of outgoing leadership, EE Division Chair Clark Nguyen was commemorated with a bobblehead in his likeness, which also showcases one of his hobbies and his favored tool of instruction in EE electronics courses: the electric guitar. Read about our Leadership Distinguished Alumi Nancy Amato, Kishore Seendripu and David Allstot Distinguished Alumni Award Ceremony 2024 The 2024 Distinguished EECS Alumni Awards honored Nancy Amato, David Allstot, Richard Muray, Lars Rasmussen, and Kishore Seendripu at a private ceremony at the Faculty Club on May 7th, 2024. Read about our Winners 2025 Eugene L. Lawler Prize winners and the EECS Chairs Student Awards 2024 Each year the EECS Student Awards Committee selects winners for each of our department awards, many based on nominations gathered from EECS students, faculty and staff. See the Winners Artist Dana King The Joseph Thomas Gier Memorial Dedication Ceremony We invite you to recognize the profound contributions of Professor Joseph Thomas Gier to the University of California, the first tenured Black professor in the UC system. Sculptor Dana King unveiled a bronze statue in honor of Gier in the Blum Hall courtyard. the UC system. Sculptor Dana King unveiled a bronze statue in honor of Gier in the Blum Hall courtyard. Watch the Program Prof. Emerita Ruzena Bajcsy The Past and Future of Robotics and Machine Intelligence Based on 250 Years of Research Experience Although renowned roboticist Ruzena Bajcsy recently retired from Berkeley, she returned to discuss her insights on how robotics research has evolved over the past half-century with five senior colleagues who have combined research experience of over 200 years.
